event: The Yenisei Kyrgyz defeated the Uyghur Khaganate, leading to the rise of Kyrgyz influence across Central Asia.
The Kyrgyz tribes were incorporated into the Mongol Empire after submitting to Genghis Khan's son Jochi.
The Kyrgyz began forming stronger tribal alliances in the Tian Shan region, developing their own political structures.
The Kyrgyz people faced conflicts with the Dzungar Khanate and defended their lands in the Tian Shan region.
After the defeat of the Dzungar Khanate by the Qing Empire, Kyrgyz tribes expanded their influence in the Tian Shan area.
The Kyrgyz territories were incorporated into the Russian Empire after the annexation of the Kokand Khanate.
The Kara-Kyrgyz Autonomous Oblast was established as part of the Soviet Union, marking the beginning of modern Kyrgyz administrative borders.
The Kyrgyz Soviet Socialist Republic was established as a full union republic within the Soviet Union.
Kyrgyzstan declared independence from the Soviet Union on August 31, becoming a sovereign state.
Kyrgyzstan experienced a political revolution that led to major changes in the country's government system.
